title: Count Array Triplets

content.problemStatement:
Given an array of integers, count all triplets where sum of any two elements equals the third element. A triplet can have any arrangement of elements (i.e., sum of first two equals third, or sum of first and third equals second, etc.).


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers




Output Format

For each test case, print the count of valid triplets.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers a1, a2, …, aN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of unordered triplets (i, j, k) with distinct indices such that the sum of any two of the three elements equals the third element.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Triplets are (1,2,3) and (2,3,5). Test case 2: No two numbers sum to a third. Test case 3: Valid triplets are (1,2,3), (1,3,4), (1,4,5) and (2,3,5).
The only unordered pair of distinct indices is the two 1's; their sum is 2, which exists once, giving one triplet.
The pair of the two 2's sums to 4, and there are two distinct indices holding 4, so two different triplets are formed.

solution.approach: For each test case we store the frequency of every value (the values are bounded by 10^5, so a simple array works). Then we iterate over all unordered pairs of indices (i, j) (i < j) and compute s = a[i] + a[j]. If s is within the allowed range we add freq[s] to the answer because each occurrence of s at a different index k forms a valid triplet (i, j, k). The pair loop runs in O(N^2) time and the frequency array uses O(MAXV) extra space. This counts each unordered triplet exactly once because we consider each unordered pair only once and the third element must be distinct from the pair (values are positive, so a[i] + a[j] cannot equal a[i] or a[j]).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case (N ≤ 1000, which easily fits the limits).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(MAXV) ≈ O(100000) for the frequency array.
